tweets.content: What's your go-to move when you arrive somewhere new? Wander aimlessly, hit the famous spots first, or ask locals for their secret recommendations?

Spring break planning stress hitting different when your group can't agree on anything? Roamly's private preference collection lets everyone be honest about what they actually want without the awkward group chat drama ✈️ #travel #buildinpublic #traveltech

Spring break timing tip: Book that shoulder season Europe trip for late April instead of peak summer. 40% cheaper flights, fewer crowds, and perfect weather. Plus you'll actually get those Instagram shots without 200 tourists in the background 📸 #travel #traveltech

Cherry blossom season in Kyoto is magical but here's the thing nobody tells you: peak bloom lasts maybe 5 days and the crowds are insane. Book for late April instead when petals are falling like snow and you can actually breathe 🌸 #travel #digitalnomad

Roamly's date coordination just saved another group from the "when can everyone go?" spiral that kills 90% of trips before they start. One click finds windows that actually work for everyone 🗓️ https://roamly.vientapps.com #travel #traveltech #buildinpublic

Just pushed a gnarly bug fix that was breaking Roamly's AI planning for groups over 8 people. Turns out I was concatenating preferences wrong and Claude was getting confused. 3am debugging sessions hit different when users are counting on you 🐛 #indiehackers #buildinpublic

The hardest part about building Roamly isn't the AI or the date coordination logic. It's deciding which features NOT to build. Users suggest amazing ideas daily but every yes is a thousand nos to something else #indiehackers #buildinpublic

Building a YouTube Short generator taught me that AI tools work best when you treat them like specialized contractors, not magic boxes. Claude writes scripts, Fal generates images, ElevenLabs does voiceover. Each does one thing well. #ai #buildinpublic #devlog

Porto's port wine cellars are caves carved into cliffsides where 200-year-old barrels age in darkness. Sip vintage tawny watching sunset paint the Douro gold. Spring = fewer crowds, perfect weather ⚡ #travel #buildinpublic #portugal

When you rebuild a client's site from scratch after an AI builder fails them, do you eat the extra hours or charge for the lesson learned? Asking for a friend who just spent 20 hours undoing vendor lock-in 😅 #webdev #indiehackers #clientwork

Roamly's no-go zones feature hits different for spring break. Tell it "skip party destinations" and watch it craft perfect alternatives. Your group gets adventure without the chaos they didn't want 🚫 #travel #buildinpublic #traveltech

Paper trading my Trump Truth Social bot worked flawlessly. Real markets? Different story. Turns out the 3-second delay between RSS feed → Claude analysis → trade execution meant I was always buying the top of moves that lasted 30 seconds 📉 #ai #trading #devlog

Just shipped PackSmart: free AI packing tool that checks real weather for your dates then tells you what to bring. No more jacket anxiety 🎒 https://vientapps.com/blog/building-packsmart #buildinpublic #ai #travel

Ever shipped something simple that unexpectedly took off? My joke extension started as "I want a clean joke in new tabs" and somehow 4,600 people installed it. What's your accidental success story? #indiehackers #chromeextension #buildinpublic

Roamly's style preferences are saving spring break groups from the "beach vs culture" death spiral. Tag yourself as Nature + Active Sports, your friend picks Beach + Nightlife, and AI builds days that actually work for everyone 🌿 #travel #buildinpublic #traveltech

jsPDF for media kit generation was the wrong choice. 60-second render times, layout nightmares, and memory leaks with images. Should've gone with Puppeteer + HTML/CSS from day one. Sometimes the boring solution is the right one 🤦‍♂️ #webdev #typescript #devlog

Mexico City's street food runs deeper than tacos. Hunt down esquites in Roma Norte, then follow locals to hole-in-wall pulquerías where fermented agave flows. Spring weather = perfect wandering without melting 🌶️ #travel #buildinpublic #mexico

Charleston's real magic hides down cobblestone alleys where 300-year-old houses lean together, whispering secrets. Spring brings jasmine evenings and porch swings creaking with stories older than America 🏛️ #travel #buildinpublic #charleston

Claude Code's `/usage` and `/context` break flow when you check them constantly. Built a bash statusline showing rate limits, context window, and reset timer as progress bars on every prompt. One glance = full picture 📊 #buildinpublic #claude #bash

Most SEO tools check if Google can crawl you. None check if ChatGPT can find you when users ask about your product. Built ShipReady to audit for answer engines. Check if your robots.txt blocks GPTBot right now https://vientapps.com/blog/building-shipready #seo #ai #buildinpublic

The 3-Hour Rule: If your multiplayer game idea takes more than 3 hours to explain the technical architecture, you're overthinking it. Firebase arrays, real-time sync, turn management - keep it simple or players bounce before the fun starts #gamedev #reactnative #buildinpublic
AI builders get you 80% there in minutes, but fixing that last 20% costs more than building from scratch. Spent 6 hours undoing LandingSite.ai's mess, rebuilt clean in 2 with Astro.

Claude Code on Bedrock blocks microphone access for security but I type at 20 WPM. Built a dead simple iOS app: tap button, speak, text hits clipboard instantly. One paste vs minutes of hunting-and-pecking #buildinpublic #reactnative #speechrecognition

BurnRate worked perfectly until I realized the API keys it needed could drain your account with one bad call. Security > convenience. Build the simple version first: spreadsheet tracking https://vientapps.com/blog/shelving-burnrate #buildinpublic #indiehackers #api

Carrier-specific bag limits aren't edge cases - they're the problem. Built size checkers with orientation-agnostic math: your 22x14x9 bag fits Delta vertically but Ryanair horizontally. One JSON, 50 airlines, zero €55 surprises #typescript #travel #buildinpublic

The Preference Paralysis Rule: Groups debating in chat take 3x longer to book and pick destinations 40% less satisfying. Collect votes privately first, discuss only top 3. Democracy works better in darkness #indiehackers #buildinpublic #travel

The 6-Decision Rule: When stuck on design direction, give AI exactly 6 "decide for me" constraints and ship whatever it builds. Claude turned my static homepage into animated flight paths in one conversation.
